origin: UAT 2026-04-12 finding #30 — fuzzy match false positive

ground_truth_policy.description: Two beneficiaries sharing an initial pattern must not be auto-linked as the same person. The engine must distinguish by full token-level comparison and resolve them to distinct person_key values.

expected_outcome.reason: Token-set Jaccard = 1/3 (only 'adeleke' shared; second tokens have Levenshtein >= 6); falls below primary-gate threshold
Fuzzy Jaccard = 1.0 (both tokens match within Levenshtein <= 2); per-token Levenshtein = 2 on olufemi/oluwafemi (insert 'wa'); genuine spelling-variant ambiguity, human decides
Name order does not affect identity; both tokens exact match; fuzzy Jaccard = 1.0 with Levenshtein = 0

expected_outcome.audit_note: Likely typo — 1-character Levenshtein on second token
expected_outcome.reason: Fuzzy Jaccard = 1.0; per-token Levenshtein = 1; single-character typo is high-confidence same-person
SEARCH is Google-suggest-style: returns all candidates matching the prefix for human selection. No auto-link implication.

engine_principles: SEARCH and MATCH are distinct operations. SEARCH returns all candidates (broad, inclusive); MATCH applies confidence bands to decide auto-link vs review vs new-person.
Fuzzy Jaccard (with per-token Levenshtein threshold <= 2) is the primary gate for MATCH. Jaccard < 0.5 => NOT_MATCH regardless of any other signal.
Above primary gate: max per-token Levenshtein determines band. 0 = HIGH (exact). 1 = HIGH_WITH_TYPO_FLAG. 2-4 = MEDIUM (review). >= 5 = NOT_MATCH.
Namesake frequency guard applies on top of HIGH band: if normalised name frequency exceeds MDA-scoped or state-scoped threshold, downgrade to MEDIUM (force review) regardless of similarity.

records_pending: Actual record IDs from UAT-era data to be populated at Story 17.4 (PersonIdentityService) kickoff. Policy above is signed-off ground truth; records fill in the input dataset.
